context.description: Read-only public NOAA SWPC solar plasma and X-ray observation snapshot.
limitations: NOAA SWPC public web observation snapshot only.
No command/control path or hazardous operating procedure.

observations.text: Use this context as a real-data seed for public observation analysis, not as live telemetry.

source.sourceLabel: NOAA SWPC RTSW and GOES X-ray 6-hour public JSON snapshot

limitations: NOAA SWPC public web observation snapshot only.
SWPC real-time endpoints are mutable; this repository stores the downloaded snapshot and raw-file digests.
OpenPlazma performs schema normalization only and does not apply independent instrument calibration.
L1 solar wind observations are not direct solar-core measurements.
GOES X-ray flux and solar wind observations do not by themselves prove fusion reaction conditions.
No command/control path or hazardous operating procedure.

observations.text: This record bundles NOAA SWPC public JSON measurements for solar wind plasma, interplanetary magnetic field, and GOES X-ray flux.
Solar wind proton density ranges from 0.12 to 11.79 cm^-3.
IMF Bz GSM ranges from -4.23 to 5.03 nT.
GOES 0.1-0.8 nm corrected X-ray flux ranges from 5.24659e-07 to 1.03966e-06 W/m^2.
The X-ray channels preserve wavelength-band and brightness variation instead of a binary light/no-light flag.
